abstract: Comprehensive guide for writing idiomatic Go table-driven tests, designed for AI agents and LLMs. Covers the core table-driven testing pattern used throughout the Go ecosystem, including struct definitions for test cases, subtest naming conventions, error handling patterns, helper functions with t.Helper(), environment guards for integration tests, and parallel test execution. Each pattern includes detailed explanations, real-world examples comparing incorrect vs. correct implementations, and specific guidance for test structure to ensure maintainability and readability.
